iti19:1.1: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
iti19:2.1:
“One thing, monks, when arising in the world, arises for the welfare of many,
for the happiness of many, for the welfare & happiness of many beings, both human & divine.
iti19:2.4:
When the Saṅgha is in concord, there are no arguments with one another,
no abuse of one another, no ganging up on one another, no abandoning of one another.
iti19:2.5:
There those with little confidence [in the teaching] become confident,
while those already confident become even more so.”
iti19:3.1: Blissful is concord in the Saṅgha.
iti19:3.4: isn’t barred from safety from bondage.
iti19:3.5: Having brought concord to the Saṅgha,
iti19:3.6: he rejoices for an eon in heaven.
